pi_items.source: Matches task ID and scripts configuration.
setup_task.sh explicitly provisions the Jupyter notebook at this path, and export_result.sh checks it.
export_result.sh explicitly checks for the generated quality report at this path.
export_result.sh explicitly checks for the repaired output CSV at this path.
export_result.sh specifically checks for the existence and size of this PNG file.
setup_task.sh injects errors into the SF dataset and saves it to this target path.
setup_task.sh saves the internal ground truth to this path for grading.
verifier.py uses this minimum count threshold to grant partial points, while actually expecting 4 specific injected issue types for full credit.
verifier.py checks if the final score is >= 60.

privileged_info_summary: The baseline dataset is a real UrbanSim Bay Area model dataset for San Francisco (152,605 buildings). `setup_task.sh` dynamically injects exactly 4 categories of data anomalies into this dataset: 1) Physical impossibility (stories > 15 but building_sqft < 3000), 2) Temporal impossibility (year_built between 2030-2099), 3) Price anomaly (residential_units > 0 but residential_sales_price = 0), and 4) Density impossibility (residential_units > 800 in buildings with stories < 5). All target output paths in the prompt strictly match the grading scripts (`export_result.sh` and `verifier.py`).
